integration_notes: The baseline is already extremely close to target, so favor incremental changes over architectural churn. Preserve the evaluator contract exactly, keep warmup/trial semantics unchanged, and do not introduce risky custom kernels or exotic torch.compile patterns. Any speed optimization must be judged against total remote runtime, not just measured trial time, because warmup already costs ~40s.
performance_task: Prioritize low-risk accuracy gains in the training/eval pipeline before touching the core architecture: tune augmentation/eval details and schedule in train_eval_code, especially stronger but cheap TTA, EMA/checkpoint averaging if nearly free, and a slightly better LR/WD/mixup-label-smoothing balance. In data_code, improve input pipeline efficiency and augmentation implementation only if it does not add startup overhead. In model_code, avoid major rewrites; only consider small-capacity or normalization tweaks if they are compile-safe and unlikely to increase step time materially.
reliability_task: Harden train_eval_code against compile/AMP/dtype issues and shape/device mismatches, since recent attempts caused runtime errors. In setup_code, keep deterministic-safe CUDA/torch settings conservative and preserve dtype consistency between normalized inputs and model parameters. In cli_code, ensure all required flags still work, preflight remains cheap, and final JSON is always emitted even on partial failures where possible.
round_goal: Push accuracy just over 94.0% without sacrificing the current ~2.6s measured trial time, while eliminating the runtime-error regressions from recent variants.
